heading: Tax on income of foreign corporations connected with United States business
label: 882 Tax on income of foreign corporations connected with United States business
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (a)(1). inserted “, 55,” after “section 11”.Pub. L. 117–1692017—Subsec. (a)(1). , which directed insertion of “or 59A,” after “section 11,”, was executed by making the insertion after “section 11” to reflect the probable intent of Congress and the amendment by . See below.Pub. L. 115–97, § 14401(d)(2)Pub. L. 115–97, § 12001(b)(14), struck out “or 1201(a)” before “on its taxable income”.Pub. L. 115–97, § 13001(b)(2)(L), struck out “, 55,” after “section 11”.Pub. L. 115–97, § 12001(b)(14)2014—Subsec. (a)(1). struck out “, 59A” after “section 11, 55”.Pub. L. 113–2951988—Subsec. (a)(1). , inserted reference to section 59A.Pub. L. 100–647, § 2001(c)(2)Subsec. (b). , inserted “, except where the context clearly indicates otherwise” after “foreign corporation”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1012(s)(2)(B)Subsec. (e). , substituted “interest on obligations of the United States which is not portfolio interest (as defined in section 881(c)(2))” for “interest on obligations of the United States”, and struck out at end “The preceding sentence shall not apply to any Guam corporation which is treated as not being a foreign corporation by section 881(b)(1) for the taxable year.”Pub. L. 100–647, § 6133(a)1986—Subsec. (a)(1). , inserted reference to section 55.Pub. L. 99–514, § 701(e)(4)(F)Subsec. (e). , inserted “The preceding sentence shall not apply to any Guam corporation which is treated as not being a foreign corporation by section 881(b)(1) for the taxable year.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1236(a)1984—Subsec. (c)(2). substituted reference to section “33” for “32” and “34” for “39”.Pub. L. 98–3691983—Subsec. (c)(2). struck out “and lubricating oil” after “gasoline”.Pub. L. 97–4241980—Subsec. (a)(3). added par. (3).Pub. L. 96–4991978—Subsec. (a). substituted in subsec. (a) heading “Imposition of tax” for “Normal tax and surtax” and in par. (1) heading “In general” for “Imposition of tax”.Pub. L. 95–6001976—Subsecs. (c)(1)(A), (2), (d). struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 94–4551966— substantially revised the income tax treatment of foreign corporations, introduced the concept of taxable income effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States into provisions dealing with the imposition of tax, substituted a concept of gross income that included gross income derived from sources within the United States not effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States and gross income effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States for a concept of gross income that included only gross income from sources within the United States, and inserted provisions for an election to treat real property income as income connected with United States business, treatment of interest on United States obligations received by banks organized in possessions, and the returns of tax by agents, and inserted cross reference to section 906(b)(1).Pub. L. 89–809

L. 99–514Pub. L. 100–647Pub. L. 99–514Pub. L. 100–647section 861 of this titleOct. 22, 1986

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A foreign corporation engaged in trade or business within the United States during the taxable year shall be taxable as provided in section 11, 55, or 59A, on its taxable income which is effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States.1So in original. The comma probably should not appear.1

children.heading: Determination of taxable income
children.label: 2 Determination of taxable income
children.text: In determining taxable income for purposes of paragraph (1), gross income includes only gross income which is effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States.

children.heading: [Cross reference ]2Par. (3) heading editorially supplied.2
children.label: 3 [Cross reference ]2Par. (3) heading editorially supplied.2
children.text: For special tax treatment of gain or loss from the disposition by a foreign corporation of a United States real property interest, see section 897.
gross income which is derived from sources within the United States and which is not effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States, and
gross income which is effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States.

children.heading: Allowance of deductions and credits
children.label: c Allowance of deductions and credits

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: In the case of a foreign corporation, the deductions shall be allowed only for purposes of subsection (a) and (except as provided by subparagraph (B)) only if and to the extent that they are connected with income which is effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States; and the proper apportionment and allocation of the deductions for this purpose shall be determined as provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Charitable contributions
children.label: B Charitable contributions
children.text: The deduction for charitable contributions and gifts provided by section 170 shall be allowed whether or not connected with income which is effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States.

children.heading: Deductions and credits allowed only if return filed
children.label: 2 Deductions and credits allowed only if return filed
children.text: A foreign corporation shall receive the benefit of the deductions and credits allowed to it in this subtitle only by filing or causing to be filed with the Secretary a true and accurate return, in the manner prescribed in subtitle F, including therein all the information which the Secretary may deem necessary for the calculation of such deductions and credits. The preceding sentence shall not apply for purposes of the tax imposed by section 541 (relating to personal holding company tax), and shall not be construed to deny the credit provided by section 33 for tax withheld at source or the credit provided by section 34 for certain uses of gasoline.

children.heading: Foreign tax credit
children.label: 3 Foreign tax credit
children.text: Except as provided by section 906, foreign corporations shall not be allowed the credit against the tax for taxes of foreign countries and possessions of the United States allowed by section 901.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: 4 Cross reference
children.text: For rule that certain foreign taxes are not to be taken into account in determining deduction or credit, see section 906(b)(1).

children.heading: Election to treat real property income as income connected with United States business
children.label: d Election to treat real property income as income connected with United States business
A
children.text: from real property located in the United States, or from any interest in such real property, including (i) gains from the sale or exchange of real property or an interest therein, (ii) rents or royalties from mines, wells, or other natural deposits, and (iii) gains described in section 631(b) or (c), and

children.label: B
children.text: which, but for this subsection, would not be treated as income effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States,

children.heading: Election after revocation, etc.
children.label: 2 Election after revocation, etc.
children.text: Paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 871(d) shall apply in respect of elections under this subsection in the same manner and to the same extent as they apply in respect of elections under section 871(d).

children.heading: Interest on United States obligations received by banks organized in possessions
children.label: e Interest on United States obligations received by banks organized in possessions

children.text: for purposes of this subpart, be treated as income which is effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States, and
shall be taxable as provided in subsection (a)(1) whether or not such corporation is engaged in trade or business within the United States during the taxable year.

children.heading: Returns of tax by agent
children.label: f Returns of tax by agent
children.text: If any foreign corporation has no office or place of business in the United States but has an agent in the United States, the return required under section 6012 shall be made by the agent.
